The Janitor doesn't like me.  Can I convince it to retest, or do I have to resubmit and ask for reviews all over again?
Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 1:

New code has landed so a rebase should resolve this issue.

Patch Set 1:

> New code has landed so a rebase should resolve this issue.

I don't think that is correct.  If there was a patch conflict, the Janitor bot would mark the patch with -1. The regular Janitor testing will not mark a patch -1 on test failures unless there are failures in newly-added tests. 

I thought this patch was just a victim of the test system move and didn't get the regular testing triggered, but in fact it looks like the builds were never done. Normally it is possible to regtrigger testing within a week of the initial build.

I could refrigerate the build on this patch, but we may as well rebase it and trigger the build that way. Simple rebase (no change to the patch itself) or editing the commit message does not invalidate the reviews.

parents.subject: LU-12275 sec: encryption with different client PAGE_SIZE

commit.subject: LU-13359 quota: call rhashtable_lookup near params decl
commit.message:
LU-13359 quota: call rhashtable_lookup near params decl

rhashtable_lookup() is an inline function which depends - for
performancs - on the 'rhashtable_params' being visible and
consnt.  So it should only be called in the same file that
declared the params.

A recent patch make pools_hash_params an external variable and calls
rhashtable_lookup from a separate file, which will break the
optimisation.

So add lov_pool_find() and use it to maintainer optimization.

Fixes: 6b9f849fd5f4 ("LU-13359 quota: make used for pool correct")
Signed-off-by: Mr NeilBrown <neilb@suse.de>
Change-Id: Ieeb3b080491f5b2c9c825885fe7a42f4a8599a2a
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/39676
Reviewed-by: James Simmons <jsimmons@infradead.org>
Reviewed-by: Sergey Cheremencev <sergey.cheremencev@hpe.com>
Reviewed-by: Petros Koutoupis <petros.koutoupis@hpe.com>
Tested-by: jenkins <devops@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <maloo@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>
